observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Order mandating the -
a) Municipal Engineer to:
i. Conduct an inspection and inventory of the road networks within
the jurisdiction of the municipality, with the assistance of the
Municipal Assessor and General Services Officer (GSO), or
personnel of the Property Unit, making sure to segregate each
road asset component, including obtaining information as to its
location and actual condition.
ii. Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network
System, adding therein the assessed values provided by the
Municipal Assessor, and copy furnish the COA, the Municipal
Accountant and the GSO/Property Unit for purposes of
reconciliation of records.

b) Municipal Assessor to:
i. Assist the Municipal Engineer, General Services Officer/Property
Unit during the conduct of inspection/inventory of the road lot
components of all road networks within the jurisdiction of the
Municipality.
ii. Assess the cost of the road network/lot components, assigning
such values to the individual items identified during the
inventory.
iii. Provide the Municipal Engineer with the assessed values on the
road network/lot components identified, for the preparation of
the inventory report.

c) General Service Officer/Property Unit personnel to prepare and
maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards based on the
details obtained from the inventory report furnished by the Municipal
Engineer.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain the Local Road
Network Ledger Cards and subsidiary ledgers with complete
description of each road asset components, and disclose such information in
the Notes to FS in the ensuing years, pursuant to the requirement under
COA Circular 2015-008.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting
entry to correct the understatement of the accumulated depreciation and
related depreciation accounts as noted in this observation, and henceforth
compute the depreciation expense based on the useful life time-table
prescribed by DPWH DO No. 176, as superseded by DO No. 225. We further
recommended that the affected asset and equity accounts be adjusted to
correct errors which persisted from prior years, including the proper
classification and recording of transactions to the appropriate asset accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mandate the immediate
inventory and appraisal of all recorded insurable assets for inclusion in the
preparation of the Property Inventory Form.

We also recommended that after the preparation of the PIF, the Municipal
General Services Officer - Designate shall file an immediate application for
insurance with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS for items of
properties owned by the municipality that are identified as insurable but were
not covered by insurance. Henceforth, the MGSO shall prepare the PIF,
listing the insurable properties/assets of the LGU at its latest appraisal
valuation, and submit such document to the COA Audit Team Leader and the
GIF-GSIS not later than April of each year.

We further recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer, by authority
from the Municipal Mayor, prepare the necessary documents and submit for
Sangguniang Bayan approval a request for the augmentation of the allocated
amount for insurance expenses in the approved budget, to cover the payment of
insurance premiums for its insurable properties during the ensuing fiscal
period. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor authorize the
inclusion in the annual budget of allocated amounts for the insurance
premium payments of all its insurable properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, along with responsible
personnel preparing the payroll, closely monitor the amounts of PhilHealth
contributions withheld and actually remitted in order to promptly correct
variances, including instances of under or over remittance.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer ensure that all PhilHealth remittances are paid within required
payment deadlines to prevent the incurrence of surcharges for interest.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer exercise conscious effort to observe the prescribed due dates set
forth by the BIR, in order to comply with the prompt remittance of withheld
taxes.

their duties pursuant to Section 444, Sub-section b.1, paragraph x of RA 7160. Thus,
require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the bank reconciliation statements for
all existing depository accounts within the prescribed timeframe and adjust the
control account and subsidiary ledgers, accordingly. Likewise, require the Municipal
Accountant to make representation with the servicing bank to clear the disparity of
the time deposit account between the accounting records of the Municipality and the
bank records.

We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant strictly
observe the timely submission of paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls/liquidation
reports and official receipts, in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1
(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39 of PD
1445.

We recommended that management require the recipient LGU – Barangays to
immediately liquidate the fund transfers, otherwise cause the return of the funds to
the Municipality. Likewise, consider the improvement of the existing clauses in the
MOA to oblige the recipient LGU-Barangay to strictly observe the accounting and
reporting requirements on the utilization and liquidation of fund transfers.
Moreover, stop the practice of giving additional fund transfers unless the previous
ones have been liquidated.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer be required to fast-tract the
implementation of programs and projects in accordance with the timelines upon
which the projects should be implemented or completed as provided for in the MOAs.
Likewise, request the Department of Agriculture – Field Office 8 to review and adjust
the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) relative to the procurement of chicken
for the Typhoon Ruby affected farmers, if warranted. Moreover, consider the return
of the unutilized funds of completed projects to the SAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator be required to implement as planned, the projects funded
under the 20% development fund, in order to attain the desired purpose of the
projects and enable the constituents to enjoy its benefits. Likewise, prioritize the
implementation of projects funded in the CYs 2016 and 2017 budget.

We recommended that henceforth, Management execute a Contract of Service or
Memorandum of Agreement when hiring Job Order (JO) personnel, stating therein
the duties and responsibilities, expected outputs and the rate per day of the workers.
The job-order personnel shall likewise be required to register their attendance
through bundy clock / biometric.

We recommended that management assign an employee to handle/operate those
equipment. Likewise, strictly enforce the provision of the contract by demanding
from the contractor for the conduct of training to would-be operators of those
equipment to attain the expected benefits and avoid further waste of government
funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to intensify collection in
order to meet, if not surpass the targeted amount of Real Property Tax Revenue.
Likewise, require the Municipal Assessor to provide the Municipal Treasurer and
Accountant with the assessment roll. On the other hand, require the Municipal
Accountant to set and record tax receivables based on valid data base or documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be directed to immediately remit the
premium contributions and loan amortizations to the Home Development Mutual
Fund and to PhilHealth, and henceforth, strictly observe the prompt remittances of
the same in accordance with Section 3(a) of the IRR of RA 9679, and Section 20(b)
of the IRR of RA 7875 as amended by RA 9241, respectively.

We recommended that management submit to the Auditor a status report on the
actions taken on the audit findings and recommendations embodied in the CY 2017
AAR using the prescribed form under COA Memorandum No. 2014-002 dated
March 18, 2014. They shall likewise furnish a copy of the said report to DBM, the
Speaker of the House of Representatives, the President of the Senate of the
Philippines, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on
Finance, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, as mandated by
Section 93 of RA 10924.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) prepare Procurement
Monitoring Report (PMR) in accordance with to Section 12.2 of the IRR of R 9184,
and furnish the COA Audit Team with a copy thereof, to serve as an effective tool in
the systematic and logical assessment/evaluation of the efficiency and effectiveness of
the Municipality’s Procurement System.

We recommended that the Municipality develop and maintain a GAD Database with
adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related
information which will serve as basis for gender analysis, planning, programming and
policy formulation.
We also recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Office
(MPDO), with the assistance of the GFPS, undertake gender analysis to identify and
confirm existing gender issues, and ensure that the GAD PPAs are responsive to the
said issues.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC- Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to recognize all the completed infrastructure projects of the municipality for calendar year 2016 to revert back to their corresponding asset accounts in order to correct the entry made. Likewise, analyze the components of the Infra assets as basis for the recognition to the Property, Plant and Equipment in the financial statements.

Assign a focal person that will consolidate and update the report the progress of the PPAs to be submitted on a quarterly basis to the Audit Team Leader.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to
transfer/record in the books of accounts the local road networks that were
recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure pursuant to COA Circular No.
2015-008 dated November 25, 2015. Likewise, require him to draw a Journal
Entry Voucher to adjust the road networks which were inadvertently closed to
Government Equity account.
To facilitate the recording of road networks in the books of accounts, the
following officials are required to extend assistance to the Municipal Accountant:
a) Municipal Engineer - To provide the Accountant and the Designated
General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation
of road components for road projects;
b) Designated-GSO - To prepare and submit a Local Road Inventory and Road
Map;
Management is enjoined to strictly follow the accounting and reporting
guidelines on the local roads asset management system prescribed by COA Circular
No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.

We reiterate our recommendation to require the Municipal Accountant
to prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) and submit the same,
together with the bank statements, credit/debit memos and paid checks. Henceforth,
strictly observe the prescribe period of its submission as provided for in COA
Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
Management should make representations with the depository bank and
request for snap shots while the bank statements are not yet available.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant be advised
to strictly observe the timely submission of the accounts in accordance with
Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006
dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend that management submit at the beginning of the year a
list of on-going and to be implemented projects/programs/activities (PPAs) for
the current year, and inform COA within ten (10) days after the award of the
infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the
appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted as
required in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommend that the concerned barangays be required to submit
liquidation reports to settle the fund transfers given to them and in no case shall
they be granted financial assistance in the ensuing year, unless the previous ones
have been fully liquidated. Henceforth, see to it that all fund transfers are
covered by a memorandum of agreement which shall stipulate among others,
the provision for: (i) the period to submit the liquidation reports by the
concerned recipient-LGUs; (ii) the return of unused funds after completion of
the project or non-implementation of the project within the allotted time frame;
and (iii) the disqualification of delinquent barangay/s from being a recipient of
LGU funds for its future projects.

We recommend that management maximize the utilization of
appropriated funds in implementing the various programs for risk reduction
and management in order to enhance disaster preparedness and response
capabilities. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant to transfer to the Special Trust Fund the unexpended balances of
DRRMF for CY 2013 to CY 2017, and the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer to prepare and submit the Report on
Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002
dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board or the Focal
Person be required to facilitate the establishment of a sanitary landfill site
pursuant to Republic Act 9003 or the “Ecological Solid Waste Management Act
of 2000.”

We recommend that the Local School Board prepare the SEF Budget
and limit the use of the fund for purposes for which it was intended pursuant to
Section 99 of RA 7160 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s.
2017 dated January 19, 2017.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer be required to
appropriate at least one percent (1%) of their IRA for LCPC programs,
projects, and activities, pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120
dated July 4, 2012.

We recommend for the immediate release of the RPT shares and
barangay aids in the total amount of P1,185,054.87 to the respective barangays
to enable the barangays to immediately appropriate the funds for
projects/programs beneficial to its constituents. Henceforth, strictly observe the
release of the share of each Barangays on a quarterly basis, within 5 days after
the end of each quarter as provided under Article 362 of the IRR of RA 7160.

We recommend that management stop the practice of paying for catering
services, meals and snacks of officials and employees during ordinary staff
meetings, conferences and SB regular sessions in order to prevent the incurrence
of unnecessary expenditures.

We recommend that management immediately remit to the Home
Development Mutual Fund (HDMF), all contributions withheld from
personnel, pursuant to Section 3 Rule VII of the IRR of RA 9679. Henceforth, stop
deducting/collecting premium contributions from personnel who are not regular
employees of the Municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to Section 74 and 114 and undertake the following: 1. Record the time investment earned of P362,249.71 to correct the understatement in Cash in Bank- Local Currency Time Deposit account; 2. Prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers and Bank Reconciliation statements so that errors could easily be detected and reconcile the balances per books against the passbook regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management ensure that all medicines and drugs procured by the municipality are covered with Certificates of Product Registration (CPR) and other permits required by law are submitted. Ensure strict compliance with the rules and regulations on procurement as provided under the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 and RA 10601 to obtain the price most advantageous to the government.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to Section 74
and 114 and undertake the following:
1. Record the time investment earned of P362,249.71 to correct the
understatement in the Cash in Bank-Local Currency Time Deposit account;
2. Prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers and Bank Reconciliation Statements
so that errors could easily be detected and reconcile the balances per books
against the passbook regularly.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to:
1. Reconcile the account Due from Other Funds with its reciprocal account
Due to Other Funds and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect
the correct balances of the accounts in the financial statements; and
2. Recognize the account Due from Other Funds of P697,500.00 in the trust
fund book for the transfer of fund to the general fund book.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the liquidation of
funds transferred to prepare Liquidation Report as approved by the Local Chief
Executive be submitted to the Department of Agriculture and to the Department of
Labor and Employment. Likewise, the unexpended balance of the completed
projects be returned to the source agency as required in COA Circular 94-013.

We recommend that the OIC-Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Vouchers
(JEV) to recognize all the completed infrastructure projects of the municipality for
calendar year 2016 to revert back to their corresponding asset accounts in order to
correct the entry made. Likewise, analyze the components of the Infra assets as basis
for the recognition to the Property, Plant and Equipment in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer conduct an inventory of the
LGUs road network system and the Accounting Unit subsequently record in its books
the amount of the road networks equivalent to the required transition provision
percentage of 25% for the calendar year 2016.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer strictly follow the guidelines in
the computation of the ABC to exclude the 7% VAT which should have been
shouldered by the contractor. Henceforth, DPWH Department Order No.22 s.2015
should be referred to in the computation of the ABC of infrastructure projects.

Working Group of the Bids and Awards Committee familiarize themselves with the
promulgations issued by the DPWH.

and/or Notice of Disallowance, as evidence warrants.

Accountant to:

- Submit the Comparison of Budget and Actual Amount presented for each
level of legislative oversight in accordance with PPSAS including its
reconciliation statement and maintain the required registries.
- Henceforth, see to it that all Obligation Requests are duly accomplished
before processing this document and the Disbursement Voucher.

facilitate the transfer of the unutilized cash balance of the LDRRMF to the
depository account in the Special Trust Fund account under the Trust
Fund books in compliance with the accounting and reporting guidelines of

and or Notice of Disallowance, as evidence warrants.

particularly on attendance of conventions of LGU officials outside of the
